instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Does BMI Affect Term Insurance?
When planning to purchase a life insurance policy, your health status can play an important role in determining the coverage amount and premiums. In this regard, your Body Mass Index (BMI) becomes a crucial factor. Insurers carefully assess various factors to understand your health risk. These include your lifestyle choices, work environment, past medical history, and even family health patterns, all of which indicate how likely you are to experience serious health issues in the future. Among these considerations, your BMI often emerges as a key indicator, offering insight into your general physical condition.
So, how exactly does BMI affect term  insurance and its premiums? Let’s know more.
What Is BMI?
BMI is short for Body Mass Index. It is a formula that determines a person’s weight relative to their height, assessing their overall health at a broad level. A good BMI indicator considers the person’s gender, age, height, and weight to determine their body mass index. A normal BMI score tends to lie anywhere between 18.5 and 24.9, indicating that a person’s weight is proportionate to his/her height.3
To calculate BMI, your body weight is divided by the square of your height. Online BMI calculators will do this for you automatically. One’s body mass can therefore be determined by how high the BMI comes out to be. One can use a BMI chart to categorize a person into any one of the following classifications2:
If BMI falls between 18.5 and 24.9, it is considered normal or healthy weight
If BMI is lower than 18.5, it is considered underweight
If BMI is higher than 25 to 29.9, it is considered overweight
If BMI is higher than 30, it is considered obese.
Now that we understand the basics of BMI, what is the relationship between BMI and term insurance plans?
How to Use a BMI Calculator?
You can compute your BMI quickly using an online BMI calculator. Follow these steps to use the calculator in just a few simple steps:1
Step 1 – Enter your height in feet and inches.
Step 2 – Enter your weight in kilograms.
Step 3 – Click the ‘Calculate’ button.
Step 4 – The BMI calculator will now display your BMI value along with the category as follows:
Underweight: less than 18.5
Normal: 18.5–24.9
Overweight: 25– 29.9
Obese: Above 30
Step 5 – Refer to the chart to confirm the exact range your BMI falls within. Knowing your category can help you anticipate how your BMI on term insurance could affect your policy. For example, a normal BMI will help you get standard premiums. On the other hand, an overweight or obese classification may require additional medical assessments or may lead to higher premiums.
Effect of BMI on Term Insurance Plans
Your Body Mass Index (BMI) can affect your term insurance in various ways. Some of these ways are as follows –
1. Additional Medical Check-ups
Does BMI affect term  insurance? If your BMI is above or below the healthy range, insurers may require detailed medical examinations to evaluate precise health risks. The medical examination report is considered for policy approval and premiums.
2. Higher premiums
If the BMI is very high, the insurance company may charge an additional premium for the higher health risk, which is indicated by a higher BMI.
3. Policy Rejection
If your BMI is extremely high and you are classified as morbidly obese, life insurance companies may reject your application. Insurers assess health risks rigorously, and an elevated BMI signals potential medical complications. Therefore, when you are considering your policy coverage, you must understand how BMI affects term  insurance approval and premiums.
Should You Buy A Term Insurance Plan If Your BMI Is High?
Regardless of your BMI, assess how much a term plan can benefit your portfolio and the security of your loved ones when deciding whether to buy one.
For those with higher than or lower than the standard/normal range of BMI scores, while your premiums might be high, your loved ones will be financially protected through a term plan in the unfortunate case of your loss. Hence, there are multiple benefits of choosing term insurance plans when planning to secure the financial future of your loved ones.
Key Takeaways
BMI on term insurance influences the pricing of your premiums. Higher or lower BMI levels often lead to additional medical evaluations or increased premiums.
Insurers may decline coverage if the applicant’s BMI indicates elevated health risks, which makes accurate disclosure of your BMI essential during underwriting.
Maintaining a stable, healthy BMI indicates a lower health (or mortality) risk, which can help you qualify for standard premiums.
Conclusion
Understanding the impact of your BMI while purchasing a term insurance plan is crucial. A higher or lower BMI may affect your premium rates. Always review your insurer’s BMI guidelines and medical requirements before adding a life insurance  plan to your financial portfolio.
FAQs
1. Can a medical examination help mitigate the impact of a high BMI on term insurance premiums?
If the BMI is high, the insurance company may require a medical check-up. If all medical parameters are good, the premium might not increase. However, in the case of a high BMI, health is considered to be at higher risk, and even after a medical check-up, the premium may be affected. This is how your BMI may impact your Term Insurance Plan.
2. What does BMI mean ?
BMI is a metric calculated based on your height and weight. Insurers use it to assess whether you fall under the underweight, normal, overweight, or obese categories.
3. Why do insurers use BMI?
Insurers use BMI as a quick health indicator to assess potential health risks, like heart disease or diabetes, determine mortality or morbidity, and set appropriate premium rates.
4. How do I know my BMI category?
Divide your weight (kg) by height squared (m²). Then check a BMI chart:1
<18.5 = underweight
18.5–24.9 = normal
25–29.9 = overweight
≥30 = obese
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Sources
